summary: This week's AI news highlights significant advancements in AI infrastructure, healthcare, and robotics. OpenAI's ChatGPT Health is now available to all US users, while Nvidia is sending GPUs to the moon. Google's massive AI spending has sparked concerns, and lawmakers are preparing an 'AI Kill Switch Act'.
insight: The increasing investment in AI infrastructure and research is driving innovation, but also raises concerns about energy consumption, data privacy, and job displacement, highlighting the need for responsible AI development and regulation.
highlights: OpenAI's ChatGPT Health is now available to all US users, allowing for personalized health insights and medical record integration.
Nvidia is expanding its presence in space with the launch of GPUs on the moon, marking a new frontier for AI-powered space exploration.
Google's AI spending has reached unprecedented levels, with the company justifying the investment as necessary for its booming cloud business.

articles.title: OpenAI makes ChatGPT Health available to all U.S. users
articles.description: Users can also integrate their personal data from services like Apple Health, Function, and MyFitnessPal.

articles.title: Runway launches AI model router as generative media gets crowded
articles.description: Runway no longer wants to be just another AI model company. It wants to become the infrastructure layer for generative media. On Thursday, the startup launched Runway Media Router

articles.title: Google just had its first negative cash flow quarter due to massive AI spending
articles.description: Google continues to report big quarterly revenue, but its AI spending has skyrocketed.

articles.title: Nvidia is sending GPUs to the moon
articles.description: If there's a place in the universe without GPUs, Nvidia is sending them there.

articles.title: AI chip startup Etched defies skeptics, hits $10.3B valuation from big-name investors
articles.description: Etched, founded by three Harvard dropouts, has created new chips and memory components that speed up inference on any AI model -- no GPUs required, it says.

articles.title: AI arms race in line for a reckoning after OpenAI hacking incident
articles.description: Aggressive training techniques sharpens threat of bad behavior by leading models.
